questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: One mole of triatomic vapours of an unknown substance effuses $\frac{4}{3}$ time faster than 1 mole of $\mathrm{O}_2$ under identical conditions. If the density of unknown vapours at pressure $\mathbf{P}$ and temperature $T$ is $d$, which of the following statement(s) is/are correct for the unknown substance?
options: Density at NTP = $0.8035 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{L}$
$z$ (compressibility factor) $=\frac{15 \mathrm{P}}{4 \mathrm{dRT}}$
Vapour density = 9
$z$ (compressibility factor) $=\frac{18 \mathrm{P}}{\mathrm{dRT}}$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{R_{\text {vap }}}{R_{\mathrm{O}_2}}=\sqrt{\frac{32}{M_{\text {vap }}}}=\frac{4}{3} \\ & \Rightarrow M_{\text {vap }}=\frac{32 \times 9}{16}=18 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{VD}=9 ; \text { Density at NTP } \\ & =\frac{18}{22.4}=0.8035 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{L} \\ & Z=\frac{P V}{n R T}=\frac{P M}{d R T}=\frac{18 \mathrm{P}}{d R T}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: According to Maxwell's distribution of molecular speeds, for the below graph drawn for two different samples of gases $A$ and $B$ at temperature $T_1$ and $T_2$ respectively, which of the following statements is/are INCORRECT:
options: If $T_1=T_2$, then molecular mass of gas $B\left(M_B\right)$ is greater than molecular mass of gas $A\left(M_A\right)$.
If molecular mass of gas $A\left(M_A\right)$ is equal to molecular mass of gas $B\left(M_B\right)$, then $T_1 \gt T_2$.
If $T_1 \lt T_2$, then molecular mass of gas $B\left(M_B\right)$ is necessarily less than molecular mass of gas $A\left(M_A\right)$.
If gas $A$ is $O_2$ and gas $B$ is $N_2$, then considering them to be ideal gases, $T_1$ is necessarily less than $T_2$.
questions.solution:
At constant temperature, decrease in molecular mass causes flattening of the graph. For same molecular mass of gas, increase in temperature causes flattening of the graph.
$\begin{aligned}
& \left(\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{mp}}\right)_{\mathrm{T}_1} \lt \left(\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{mp}}\right)_{\mathrm{T}_2} \\
& \therefore \frac{\mathrm{~T}_1}{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{A}}} \lt \frac{\mathrm{T}_2}{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{B}}} \quad \therefore \frac{\mathrm{~T}_2}{\mathrm{~T}_1} \gt \frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{B}}}{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{A}}}
\end{aligned}$
But, $\frac{M_B}{M_A}$ can be less than or greater than 1
Similarly,$\frac{M_A}{M_B} \gt \frac{T_1}{T_2}$
If gas $A$ is $O_2$ and gas $B$ is $N_2$, then $M_A \gt M_B$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If temperature of a gas is raised, which of the following would be true?
options: Fraction of the molecules possessing most probable velocity will increase
Fraction of the molecules possessing most probable velocity will decrease
Fraction of possessing very low velocity will decrease
Fraction possessing very high velocity will increase
questions.solution: On increasing temperature, the Maxwell curve of distribution of molecular velocity is flattened and maximum is shifted to higher velocity.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
For a sample of an ideal gas, the maxwell distribution curve of molecular speed is given as :
Select the correct statements.
options: Most probable speed at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is $\mathrm{V}_1$
Most probable speed at $127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is $\sqrt{\frac{4}{3}} \mathrm{~V}_1$
$\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{mms}}$ at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}=\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{~V}_1$
$\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{ms}}$ at $127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~V}_1$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & V_{m p} \text { at } 127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}=\sqrt{\frac{400}{300}} V_1=\sqrt{\frac{4}{3}} V_1 \\ & V_{r m s} \text { at } 27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}=\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} V_1 \\ & V_{m s} \text { at } 127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}=\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}} \times \sqrt{\frac{4}{3}} \mathrm{~V}_1=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~V}_1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A certain gas has the equation of state, $\mathrm{P}=\frac{\mathrm{RT}}{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{m}}-\mathrm{bT}}-\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{m}}^3}$. Where a and b are constants characteristic of the gas. Choose the incorrect relationship(s).
[ $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{c}}, \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{c}}$ and $\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{c}}$ are critical pressure, volume and temperature respectively]
options: $\mathbf{a}=\frac{\mathbf{R T}_c \mathbf{V}_c^2}{3}$
$b=\frac{V_c}{2 T_c}$
$\mathrm{a}=\frac{4 \mathrm{R} \mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{e}} \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{c}}}{3}$
$\mathbf{b}=\frac{\mathbf{V}_{\mathrm{c}}}{3 \mathrm{~T}_{\mathrm{c}}}$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & P=\frac{R T}{V_m-b T}-\frac{a}{V_m^3} \\ & \left(\frac{d P}{d V_m}\right)_T=-\frac{R T}{\left(V_m-b T\right)^2}+\frac{3 a}{V_m^4}\end{aligned}$
Under critical condition
$\left(\frac{d P}{d V_m}\right)_T=-\frac{R T}{\left(V_m-b T\right)^2}+\frac{3 a}{V_m^4}=0$   ...(i)
$\left(\frac{d^2 P}{d V_m^2}\right)_T=+\frac{2 R T}{\left(V_m-b T\right)^3}-\frac{12 a}{V_m^5}=0$   ...(ii)
On solving (i) and (ii), we get
$a=\frac{4 R T_c V_c^2}{3}$ and $b=\frac{V_c}{2 T_c}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Gas A $(\mathrm{mol}$. Mass $=16)$ and gas B $(\mathrm{mol}$. Mass $=4)$ are confined in to a vessel at partial pressure of 2 atm each at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The gases are then allowed to effuse out at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ through a valve till the pressure inside the vessel becomes 2 atm after which the valve is closed and the gases are heated to $127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and allowed to react as $\mathrm{A}+\mathrm{B} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}$ for which $\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{p}}=0.1 \mathrm{~atm}^{-1}$. By assuming that C is not formed till the effusion process is completed, Identify the correct statements in the following
options: The no. of moles of A and B diffused out are in the ratio 1:2.
The no. of moles of A and B left after diffusion at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ are in the ratio $2: 1$
Partial pressure of B at $127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is $\frac{8}{9} \mathrm{~atm}$.
Partial pressure of C at $127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is $\frac{1}{8} \mathrm{~atm}$
questions.solution:
At $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, total pressure $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{A}}+\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{B}}=2+2=4 \mathrm{~atm}$
$$
\frac{r_A}{r_B}=\frac{n_A}{n_B}=\frac{P_A}{P_B} \sqrt{\frac{M_B}{M_A}} \Rightarrow \frac{n_A}{n_B}=\frac{1}{2}
$$
$\therefore$ moles diffused out at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ are in the ratio $1: 2$
Initial moles a a
moles left $a-x \quad a-2 x$
Total moles after diffusion $=a-x+a-2 x=a$
( $\because$ P drops from 4 atm to $2 \mathrm{~atm} \Rightarrow$ moles should decrease from 2 a to a)
$\therefore \mathrm{a}=3 \mathrm{x}$
$\therefore$ After diffusion moles of $A$ and $B$ are $3 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x}=2 \mathrm{x}$ and $3 \mathrm{x}-2 \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{x}$
$\therefore$ moles of $A$ and $B$ left are in the ratio $2: 1$
$\therefore \quad$ Partial pressures after diffusion at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
For A it is $\frac{2}{3} \times \mathrm{P}=\frac{2}{3} \times 2=\frac{4}{3}$
For $B$ it is $\frac{1}{3} \times \mathrm{P}=\frac{1}{3} \times 2=\frac{2}{3}$
When the vessel is heated to $127^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$,
Partial pressure of $\mathrm{A}=\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{4}{3}=\frac{16}{9}$ atm $\left(\because \frac{\mathrm{P}_1}{\mathrm{~T}_1}=\frac{\mathrm{P}_2}{\mathrm{~T}_2}\right)$
Partial pressure of $B=\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{4}{3}=\frac{8}{9} \mathrm{~atm}$
Now
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{A} \\ & \begin{array}{l}\mathrm{16} \\ \frac{16}{9}\end{array} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{~B} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}}{9}-\mathrm{P} \\ & \frac{16}{9} \quad \frac{8}{9}-\mathrm{P} \quad \mathrm{P} \\ & \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{P}}=0.1=\frac{\mathrm{P}}{\left(\frac{16}{9}-\mathrm{P}\right)\left(\frac{8}{9}-\mathrm{P}\right)} \Rightarrow \text { partial pressure of } \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{P}=0.126 \mathrm{~atm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
